rulings.Case Title: JOHN BURNS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 21, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Defendant SVI Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Stockham or ¿Stockholm¿ valves) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Burns¿ declaration are overruled. The court gave the benefit of the doubt to plaintiff and has treated all references in plaintiff¿s deposition to ¿Stockholm¿ as referring to ¿Stockham.¿ =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY HORSMAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 21, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 14. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence) and 2 (strict liability). Plaintiff presented evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Horsman¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his false representation claim. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE MICHAEL SOTCAN et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 21, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Pfizer, Inc.¿s motion to change venue on the ground of improper venue is denied. The motion to change venue on the ground of convenience of the witnesses and ends of justice is continued to October 6, 2010 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220. Plaintiffs¿ motion to strike the declarations submitted with the reply papers is denied. Venue is proper in this county because at least one defendant resides in San Francisco County. To facilitate the determination of whether defendant¿s motion should be granted based on convenience of the witnesses and ends of justice, plaintiffs are required to disclose no later than September 30 the names and last known addresses of each person presently known to plaintiffs who has knowledge of any facts supporting plaintiffs¿ claims against each defendant and the facts known by that person. The prohibition on ex parte contacts with a plaintiff¿s physicians does not extend to contacts regarding matters other than the plaintiff¿s physical condition such as convenience of the location of testifying at trial. Plaintiffs may file supplemental opposition papers no later than September 30. Defendant may file supplemental reply papers no later than October 4. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN ROBINSON VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 21, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 16. Defendant SSW, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Whether Corporations Code §2010 applies to non-California corporations is unclear. There is a conflict in decisions of the California Courts of Appeal and the issue is currently pending before the California Supreme Court. This court believes that the better-reasoned view is that §2010 only applies to California corporations. Even if §2010 applies to defendant and thus plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant are not barred because defendant was sued after expiration of the Nebraska survival period for dissolved corporations, defendant sustained its burden and plaintiffs have not presented sufficient evidence that Mr. Robinson was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)
